intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer infinite scroll with minimal decision points, reducing friction when browsing recipes
facade.label: One long scroll
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into continuous feed; recipe cards stack vertically without pagination; minimize 'load more' moments
facade.rejectImplies: Explore paginated or tabbed navigation; user may want clear stopping points and category segmentation

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer infinite scroll with minimal decision points, reducing friction when browsing recipes
evidence.implication: Lean into continuous feed; recipe cards stack vertically without pagination; minimize 'load more' moments

facade.hypothesis: A bright, energetic interface with fast visual feedback rewards the user's desire to minimize cooking friction—making the app feel like a helpful friend, not a chore
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, calm vs energetic
facade.label: Cheerful and quick
facade.acceptImplies: User wants the app to feel encouraging and zippy—design should use vibrant accent colors, snappy animations, and celebratory feedback (confetti, upbeat copy)
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a more neutral, no-nonsense tone—design should be clean and straightforward, letting recipes be the focus without emotional theater

facade.hypothesis: Users feel less overwhelmed by a pre-filled ingredient list if items animate in sequentially rather than load all at once
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — Animated progression vs instant load
facade.label: Ingredients appear one by one
facade.acceptImplies: Staggered, gentle reveals reduce friction for people intimidated by cooking; build toward motion-forward interaction patterns
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer instant clarity over animation; motion may feel condescending or slow; move toward snappy, instant-load patterns

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking want personality and permission to be imperfect, so casual, human typography—not sterile sans-serif—signals this is a low-pressure, forgiving space.
facade.label: Messy handwriting font
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace loose, human, imperfect visual language throughout the app—scribbled notes, casual imagery, conversational copy.
facade.rejectImplies: Keep the interface clean, organized, and polished—users want the app to compensate for cooking chaos, not amplify it.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to irreverent, joke-filled voice than earnest encouragement
facade.label: Sarcastic tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into humor, self-deprecation, and witty copy throughout the app—make fun of cooking complexity, celebrate shortcuts
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with supportive, straightforward tone—focus on making cooking easy without winking at the user

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to visual recognition of ingredients than reading lists
facade.axisTargeted: Charts vs text — visual vs linguistic representation of recipe data
facade.label: Ingredient photos instead of text
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into image-heavy ingredient selection, photo-based substitution guides, visual prep steps over written instructions
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize concise text labels, ingredient names as scannable lists, minimize decorative food photography in favor of clarity

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, skill level, time) vs. simple discovery with minimal decision friction
axes.poleA: Lots of filters or keep it simple

axes.label: Whether user wants satisfying progress feedback and celebration vs. invisible, frictionless interactions that get out of the way

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, meditative cooking experience vs. fast-paced, time-saving urgency and efficiency focus
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, skill level, time) vs. simple discovery with minimal decision friction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants satisfying progress feedback and celebration vs. invisible, frictionless interactions that get out of the way
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users overwhelmed by cooking complexity prefer generous whitespace and gentle visual separation between recipes, reducing cognitive load and making browsing feel less demanding
facade.label: Soft cards, spacious
facade.acceptImplies: Design with card-based layout, breathing room between elements, minimal visual clutter; prioritize calm over information density
facade.rejectImplies: Users can tolerate or prefer compact layouts; density and visual efficiency matter more than psychological comfort; iterate toward grid-heavy or list-heavy alternatives

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in under 15 minutes with minimal ingredients and zero fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so easy, you'll actually cook them

Filter by time
Today's picks
Buttered Toast with Egg
⏱ 4 min
🛒 3 ingredients
Pasta Aglio e Olio
⏱ 8 min
🛒 4 ingredients
Avocado & Lime Rice Bowl
⏱ 6 min
🛒 5 ingredients
